steps.description: Audit and repair the canonical database-layer implementation under api/db so it is self-contained, compiles cleanly, and remains the source of truth. This includes fixing the broken seed script, confirming the migration runner/bootstrap wiring, and ensuring the pool client implements connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, and graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown without introducing new TypeScript/runtime errors.

Implement and reconcile the canonical schema assets under api/db so schema.sql and the initial migration represent the required 'everything is a document' PostgreSQL model, include all required tables/enums/extensions/triggers/indexes, and are internally consistent with the seed/runtime expectations. Preserve api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql as the initial canonical migration and ensure schema.sql fully mirrors the intended fresh-db schema.

Fix bootstrap and legacy wrapper behavior so runtime and test imports that still target api/src/db/* are thin wrappers/re-exports only after the canonical api/db implementation exists, and ensure bootstrap resolves schema.sql/migrations from the canonical api/db path rather than leaving broken or duplicated logic under api/src/db. This step should also remove the malformed mixed-content state currently present in api/src/db/seed.ts and verify wrappers stay minimal.

Update package/build asset wiring so builds and downstream tooling copy/use the canonical api/db schema and migrations instead of the legacy src/db asset path. This prevents build-time or deploy-time breakage and aligns the package with the user's path constraint.

Revise direct documentation/script references in the repo that currently point users or deploy flows at api/src/db/schema.sql or the old db entrypoints, so the canonical path is api/db/*. Keep changes focused to files that explicitly reference the old path or migration/seed commands and could mislead future use.

fileEdits.new_string: export { ensureDatabaseSchema } from '../../db/bootstrap.js';
export { pool } from '../../db/client.js';
export * from '../../db/migrate.js';
export * from '../../db/seed.js';

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,","total_lines":589}

   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',

   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194	
   195	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON documents;
   196	CREATE TRIGGER prevent_circular_parent_trigger
   197	BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF parent_id ON documents
   198	FOR EACH ROW
   199	EXECUTE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent();
   200	
   201	-- Relationship type enum for document associations
   202	DO $$
   203	BEGIN
   204	  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_type WHERE typname = 'relationship_type') THEN
   205	    CREATE TYPE relationship_type AS ENUM ('parent', 'project', 'sprint', 'program');
   206	  END IF;
   207	END
   208	$$;
   209	
   210	-- Document associations junction table (replaces direct relationship columns)
   211	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_associations (
   212	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   213	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   214	  related_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   215	  relationship_type relationship_type NOT NULL,
   216	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   217	  metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   218	
   219	  -- Prevent duplicate associations of the same type
   220	  CONSTRAINT unique_association UNIQUE (document_id, related_id, relationship_type),
   221	
   222	  -- Prevent self-references
   223	  CONSTRAINT no_self_reference CHECK (document_id != related_id)
   224	);
   225	
   226	-- Document history (audit trail for all document field changes)
   227	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_history (
   228	  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   229	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   230	  field TEXT NOT NULL,
   231	  old_value TEXT,
   232	  new_value TEXT,
   233	  changed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   234	  automated_by TEXT,  -- Identifies automated change source (e.g., "claude")
   235	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   236	);
   237	
   238	-- Document snapshots (preserves state before type conversions for undo)
   239	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS document_snapshots (
   240	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   241	  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   242	
   243	  -- Snapshot of document state at time of conversion
   244	  document_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   245	  title TEXT NOT NULL,
   246	  properties JSONB,
   247	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Preserved for issues
   248	
   249	  -- Metadata
   250	  snapshot_reason VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'conversion',  -- 'conversion', 'manual'
   251	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   252	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
   253	);
   254	
   255	-- API tokens for CLI/external tool authentication
   256	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_tokens (
   257	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   258	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   259	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   260	  name TEXT NOT NULL,         -- User-provided name (e.g., "Claude Code")
   261	  token_hash TEXT NOT NULL,   -- SHA-256 hash (never store plain token)
   262	  token_prefix TEXT NOT NULL, -- First 8 chars for identification
   263	  last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   264	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,     -- NULL = never expires
   265	  revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,     -- NULL = active, timestamp = revoked
   266	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   267	
   268	  UNIQUE(user_id, workspace_id, name)
   269	);
   270	
   271	-- Sprint iterations (tracking work progress per sprint)
   272	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sprint_iterations (
   273	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   274	  sprint_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   275	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   276	  story_id TEXT,
   277	  story_title TEXT NOT NULL,
   278	  status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pass', 'fail', 'in_progress')),
   279	  what_attempted TEXT,
   280	  blockers_encountered TEXT,
   281	  author_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   282	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   283	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
   284	);
   285	
   286	-- Issue iterations (tracking work progress per issue)
   287	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issue_iterations (
   288	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   289	  issue_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   290	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   291	  status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pass', 'fail', 'in_progress')),
   292	  what_attempted TEXT,
   293	  blockers_encountered TEXT,
   294	  author_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   295	  created

-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
-- Multi-Workspace Architecture

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;

-- Workspaces
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));

-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,

{"success":true,"content":"     1	/**
     2	 * Welcome to Ship - Tutorial Document
     3	 *
     4	 * This is the comprehensive onboarding document shown to new users.
     5	 * Used by both seed.ts (dev data) and setup.ts (production first-run).
     6	 */
     7	
     8	export const WELCOME_DOCUMENT_TITLE = 'Welcome to Ship';
     9	
    10	export const WELCOME_DOCUMENT_CONTENT = {
    11	  type: 'doc',
    12	  content: [

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Migration 005: Create missing person documents
     2	-- For users who have workspace memberships but no corresponding person document
     3	-- This handles the case where users existed before person documents were mandatory
     4	
     5	-- Step 1: Create person documents for users who don't have one in each workspace
     6	INSERT INTO documents (workspace_id, document_type, title, properties, created_by)
     7	SELECT
     8	  wm.workspace_id,
     9	  'person',
    10	  u.name,
    11	  jsonb_build_object('user_id', u.id::text, 'email', u.email),
    12	  u.id
    13	FROM workspace_memberships wm
    14	JOIN users u ON wm.user_id = u.id
    15	LEFT JOIN documents d ON d.workspace_id = wm.workspace_id
    16	  AND d.document_type = 'person'
    17	  AND d.properties->>'user_id' = u.id::text
    18	WHERE d.id IS NULL;
    19	
    20	-- Step 2: Also backfill any existing person documents that might have NULL user_id
    21	-- Match by title = user name (case-insensitive) within same workspace
    22	UPDATE documents d
    23	SET properties = d.properties || jsonb_build_object(
    24	  'user_id', u.id::text,
    25	  'email', u.email
    26	)
    27	FROM users u
    28	JOIN workspace_memberships wm ON wm.user_id = u.id
    29	WHERE d.workspace_id = wm.workspace_id
    30	  AND d.document_type = 'person'
    31	  AND LOWER(d.title) = LOWER(u.name)
    32	  AND (d.properties->>'user_id' IS NULL OR d.properties->>'user_id' = '');
    33	
    34	-- Step 3: Log results
    35	DO $$
    36	DECLARE
    37	  total_memberships INTEGER;
    38	  total_person_docs INTEGER;
    39	  docs_with_user_id INTEGER;
    40	BEGIN
    41	  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO total_memberships FROM workspace_memberships;
    42	  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO total_person_docs FROM documents WHERE document_type = 'person';
    43	  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO docs_with_user_id FROM documents WHERE document_type = 'person' AND properties->>'user_id' IS NOT NULL AND properties->>'user_id' != '';
    44	
    45	  RAISE NOTICE 'Workspace memberships: %, Person documents: %, With user_id: %', total_memberships, total_person_docs, docs_with_user_id;
    46	END $$;
    47	","total_lines":47}

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	
   124	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   125	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   126	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   127	
   128	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194	
   195	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON documents;
   196	CREATE TRIGGER prevent_circular_parent_trig

It runs without a user present, authenticated via a workspace-scoped API token.

/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/FLEETGRAPH.md:52:FleetGraph determines ownership from Ship document properties: `assignee_id` for issues, `owner_id` for sprints and projects. Workspace membership is resolved from the `workspace_memberships` table for broadcast targeting. Role context comes from the RACI fields on document properties (owner_id, accountable_id).
/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/FLEETGRAPH.md:56:The UI sends the current page's `entityType` and `entityId` when invoking on-demand analysis or chat. The graph uses these to fetch entity-specific context: issue details + children for issues, sprint context + issues for sprints, project document + associations for projects, and explicit workspace scope when the current screen has no entity. On-demand accountability reads are actor-scoped: FleetGraph answers from the requesting user's visible action items and manager follow-up items rather than a generic service-account perspective. Chat questions and conversation history are threaded into the LLM prompt so the model reasons about what the user is asking in the context of what they are looking at.
/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/FLEETGRAPH.md:75:When the user is on a page without a specific entity context (dashboard, settings, search results), the chat falls back to workspace scope. In workspace scope, FleetGraph fetches active sprints plus the current actor's visible accountability state, including overdue personal items and manager follow-up items for direct reports. This provides a high-level summary of what needs attention across the workspace without collapsing to a generic project view.
/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/FLEETGRAPH.md:79:Each workspace keeps one active Ship Chat thread for the current user. The visible scope can change as the user moves between issue, sprint, project, and workspace views, but the floating chat does not reset on those scope changes. The history is threaded into the prompt so follow-up questions keep their context while each turn still carries the latest page context and entity hint.
/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/FLEETGRAPH.md:81:Sensitive requests are denied immediately instead of entering tool or model execution. FleetGraph declines requests for secrets, credentials, env vars, database access, deployment internals, and infrastructure internals. Chat model steps also run with a bounded timeout so the UI can fail fast instead of hanging on "Analyzing workspace...".

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	
   124	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   125	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   126	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   127	
   128	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194	
   195	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON documents;
   196	CREATE TRIGGER prevent_circular_parent_trig

   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role workspace_role NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role workspace_role NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Issue status enum (stored in document properties, but canonicalized here for migrations/validation)
   108	DO $$ BEGIN
   109	  CREATE TYPE issue_state AS ENUM ('backlog', 'triage', 'todo', 'in_progress', 'in_review', 'done', 'cancelled');
   110	EXCEPTION
   111	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   112	END $$;
   113	
   114	-- Workspace role enum
   115	DO $$ BEGIN
   116	  CREATE TYPE workspace_role AS ENUM ('admin', 'member');
   117	EXCEPTION
   118	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   119	END $$;
   120	
   121	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   122	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   123	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   124	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   125	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   126	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   127	  title_normalized TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (LOWER(title)) STORED,
   128	
   129	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   130	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   131	
   132	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   133	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   134	
   135	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   136	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   137	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   138	
   139	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   140	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   141	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   142	
   143	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   144	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   145	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   146	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   147	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   148	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   149	
   150	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   151	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   152	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   153	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   154	
   155	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   156	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   157	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   158	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   159	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   160	
   161	  -- Document conversion tracking
   162	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   163	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   164	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   165	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   166	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   167	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   168	
   169	  -- Timestamps
   170	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   171	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   172	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   173	
   174	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   175	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   176	
   177	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   178	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   179	);
   180	
   181	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   182	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   183	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   184	DECLARE
   185	  current_parent UUID;
   186	  depth INT := 0;
   187	  max_depth INT := 100;
   188	BEGIN
   189	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   190	    RETURN NEW;
   191	  END IF;
   192	  IF NEW.id IS NOT NULL AND NEW.parent_id = NEW.id THEN
   193	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be its own parent', NEW.id;
   194	  END IF;
   195	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   196	    RETURN NEW;
   197	  END IF;
   198	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   199

    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	
   124	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   125	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   126	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   127	
   128	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194	
   195	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON documents;
   196	CREATE TRIGGER prevent_circular_parent_trig

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role workspace_role NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role workspace_role NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Issue status enum (stored in document properties, but canonicalized here for migrations/validation)
   108	DO $$ BEGIN
   109	  CREATE TYPE issue_state AS ENUM ('backlog', 'triage', 'todo', 'in_progress', 'in_review', 'done', 'cancelled');
   110	EXCEPTION
   111	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   112	END $$;
   113	
   114	-- Workspace role enum
   115	DO $$ BEGIN
   116	  CREATE TYPE workspace_role AS ENUM ('admin', 'member');
   117	EXCEPTION
   118	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   119	END $$;
   120	
   121	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   122	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   123	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   124	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   125	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   126	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   127	  title_normalized TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (LOWER(title)) STORED,
   128	
   129	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   130	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   131	
   132	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   133	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   134	
   135	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   136	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   137	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   138	
   139	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   140	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   141	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   142	
   143	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   144	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   145	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   146	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   147	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   148	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   149	
   150	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   151	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   152	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   153	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   154	
   155	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   156	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   157	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   158	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   159	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   160	
   161	  -- Document conversion tracking
   162	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   163	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   164	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   165	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   166	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   167	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   168	
   169	  -- Timestamps
   170	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   171	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   172	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   173	
   174	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   175	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   176	
   177	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   178	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   179	);
   180	
   181	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   182	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   183	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   184	DECLARE
   185	  current_parent UUID;
   186	  depth INT := 0;
   187	  max_depth INT := 100;
   188	BEGIN
   189	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   190	    RETURN NEW;
   191	  END IF;
   192	  IF NEW.id IS NOT NULL AND NEW.parent_id = NEW.id THEN
   193	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be its own parent', NEW.id;
   194	  END IF;
   195	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   196	    RETURN NEW;
   197	  END IF;
   198	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   199

messages.content:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.

<plan>
[
  {
    "index": 0,
    "description": "Inspect and replace the legacy database package entrypoints with canonical implementations under api/db/, while preserving compatibility for existing runtime consumers via thin wrappers only after the new files exist. This includes moving/rewiring client, bootstrap/migration loading paths, seed, migrate, SSL helper, and welcome document support so api/db/* becomes the source of truth.",

      "/Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260326/api/package.json",
